root_cause_analysis: Spec arithmetic error in NEW_STR structure summary. Spec text says '22 new lines: 1 blank + ===== + header + ===== + 1 blank + 14-line body + 1 blank + closing =====' which sums to 1+1+1+1+1+14+1+1=21, not 22. Additionally the body in the explicit NEW_STR rendering is 13 lines (counting [OQ-...] header + 12 prose), not 14. Net new lines from explicit NEW_STR (24 lines) minus OLD_STR (3 lines) = 21. Therefore 1900 + 21 = 1921, not 1922.
content_correctness: All other STEP 3 invariants PASS: OQ token unique (1), header unique (1), tail line is closing separator, post-SHA != pre-SHA. The pasted content is bit-correct relative to the substrate cmb_open_question_entry.txt. Only the line-count arithmetic in the spec is off-by-one.
no_rollback_attempted: Per spec instruction 'DO NOT attempt automatic re-paste under any halt -- operator validates + re-dispatches', the modified CMB.txt is preserved as-is at post-SHA 723E9C60..C3B0. Operator validates content + decides whether to (a) accept 1921-line state and amend spec, (b) manually add one extra blank line to reach 1922, or (c) revert and re-fire with corrected spec.
secondary_anomaly_l1899_anchor: Spec OLD_STR specifies L1899 as '  ' (2 spaces). Byte-level inspection of pre-paste CMB.txt showed L1899 was a bare empty line (sequence 66 0A 0A 3D = 'f\n\n='). STEP 1 anchors only check L1898 + L1900, not L1899, so the mismatch was not caught by the spec's STEP 1 procedure. Executer corrected OLD_STR to bare empty line (matching actual file content) and applied the same correction to the corresponding line in NEW_STR. Documented as judgment call J1 in handoff.
